errors.title: Straw Man Attack
errors.explanation: A debater changes or exaggerates the other side's argument into something easier to attack.
errors.simpleExplanation: "You changed our idea into something more extreme."
exampleLines: "So you want students to stop learning completely?"

errors.title: Hasty Generalization
errors.explanation: A debater uses one or a few examples to make a conclusion about everyone or everything.
errors.simpleExplanation: "One example does not prove the whole situation."
exampleLines: "My friend hates reading, so reading is useless."

errors.title: False Cause
errors.explanation: A debater says one thing caused another thing without enough proof.
errors.simpleExplanation: "Just because two things happen together does not mean one caused the other."
exampleLines: "Students started using tablets, and grades dropped, so tablets caused bad grades."

errors.title: Slippery Slope
errors.explanation: A debater says a small action will lead to a huge disaster without proving it.
errors.simpleExplanation: "They jumped from a small problem to a huge result."
exampleLines: "If students skip one homework assignment, soon nobody will study anymore."

errors.title: Black-or-White Thinking
errors.explanation: A debater pretends there are only two choices when there are actually many possibilities.
errors.simpleExplanation: "The issue is not only one side or the other."
exampleLines: "Either students do community service, or they become selfish."

errors.title: Circular Reasoning
errors.explanation: A debater repeats the same idea instead of actually proving it.
errors.simpleExplanation: "They repeated the claim instead of explaining it."

errors.title: Overstatement / Exaggeration
errors.explanation: A debater makes the argument too extreme by using words like "always," "never," or "everyone."

errors.title: Missing Link
errors.explanation: A debater skips important reasoning steps between the claim and the conclusion.
errors.simpleExplanation: "They did not explain how Point A leads to Point B."
exampleLines: "Debate improves speaking, so students will become future leaders."
